title: Epic Lyngen Alps: Fjord One-Shot Drone
creator: Above Horizon (YouTube channel: @above.horizon)
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (fjord and alpine-lake landscapes) -- not the pack's original single worked example (that role is bbc-earth-aerial.json, per spec AC3.4), but built to the exact same rigor/honesty standard. Independently re-verified this session (not merely trusting the prior agent's candidate JSON verbatim) via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/TNAgtUC-cQs&format=json), which returned title="Epic Lyngen Alps: Fjord One-Shot Drone", author_name="Above Horizon", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@above.horizon", type="video", provider_name="YouTube" -- matching the candidate record's title/creator field-for-field, so this session's own primary-source check confirms rather than merely restates the prior agent's claim. Note one small discrepancy worth flagging honestly: the candidate JSON's creator handle was written as "@AboveHorizon", but the oEmbed-returned author_url is actually "@above.horizon" (lowercase, with a literal dot) -- the channel name itself ("Above Horizon") matches exactly, only the handle's exact casing/punctuation differs, so this is recorded here as the oEmbed-verified handle rather than the candidate's version. A second WebFetch of the live Shorts page itself (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/TNAgtUC-cQs) was attempted this session specifically to check for a Creative Commons marking or duration; it returned only a truncated stub with no extractable license text, duration, or description -- consistent with the prior agent's note that Google's bot-detection blocks direct watch-page fetches of this kind. A third WebFetch of the channel page (https://www.youtube.com/@above.horizon) was attempted to look for subscriber count, bio, or stated drone equipment/resolution, but also returned only a truncated stub with no extractable content. A WebSearch this session ("Above Horizon" drone YouTube channel Lyngen Alps fjord) surfaced only this same video's own title as a search result plus unrelated third-party Lyngenfjord drone-tourism/stock-footage pages (Drone Alps, AirVuz, Seeking The Now, Envato, Nature Relaxation) -- no creator interview, press coverage, subscriber count, or channel bio was found anywhere in this session's research, so no independent secondary-source corroboration of this creator's reach or equipment exists. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved' because no explicit Creative Commons marking was confirmed on this specific upload (per this project's stated default: assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise) -- consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field for this URL, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session; the URL path itself (/shorts/) structurally implies a sub-60s runtime by platform definition, but the exact cut point is unverified, so rather than fabricate a plausible number this field stays unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this reads as an independent drone-content channel's Shorts upload with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's research -- inventing one here would be fabrication. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's documented 0.72-0.88 band and closely tracking its other unvetted-independent-creator exemplars (e.g. wanderpeaks-langhe-autumn-vineyards.json at 0.73/0.72/0.72): sharpness=0.74 (thumbnail_url and oEmbed metadata confirm a genuine drone aerial capture of a fjord/alpine-lake reveal, a modestly positive genre signal, but no drone model, resolution, or export setting is disclosed anywhere in the title or the (truncated) channel page, so this is not lifted meaningfully above the pack's unvetted-creator floor); exposure=0.73 (fjord/alpine scenes of this kind typically involve large water-to-rock-to-sky dynamic range, similar in difficulty to other landscape exemplars in this pack, with no HDR/log-profile claim found to justify scoring above that median); motion_smoothness=0.75 (scored slightly above the pack's unvetted-creator floor because the video's own title explicitly frames it as a continuous 'one-shot' reveal, which -- even though not independently confirmed and not treated as a genuine editorial measurement, per the honesty reasoning in editorial_style.pacing_notes below -- is at minimum evidence the creator intended and marketed a single smooth continuous camera move rather than a rapid-cut compilation, a mild directional signal for smoothness specifically that doesn't apply to sharpness or exposure). `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.74, exposure=0.73, motion_smoothness=0.75, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (resolved to weights_version='default-v2', 0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition) via `.venv/bin/python3`, returning composite_score=0.7399999999999999 (rounded to 0.74 here). Because `composition` is `None`, `compute_composite_score` skips the composition term entirely (its weight-or-score-is-None guard), so the result reduces to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.74+0.73+0.75)/3 = 0.74 exactly, matching the function's actual return value. `editorial_style` reflects a genuine, and repeated, attempt rather than a shortcut: a prior agent in this task chain reported the claude-in-chrome browser extension was not connected in that environment, so no live playback/scrubbing of the video was possible, and that its own WebFetch, WebSearch, and noembed.com oEmbed-proxy fallback attempts all came up empty of any cut-count/shot-length/transition/pacing description. This session repeated an independent WebFetch of the Shorts page and the channel page (both truncated stubs, no extractable content) and an independent WebSearch (no creator commentary found), and reached the same conclusion independently rather than merely accepting the prior agent's report. Given none of these attempts, across two separate sessions, surfaced any genuine editorial observation, `review_method` stays 'not_reviewed' and `format`/`estimated_cut_count`/`avg_shot_length_seconds`/`transition_styles_observed` all stay null/empty -- the video's own promotional title claiming a 'one-shot' is explicitly NOT treated as a genuine cut-count/continuity observation (this genre often disguises match-cuts within a nominal 'one shot'), consistent with the prior agent's stated reasoning.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Live playback review was not performed in this session or the prior one: a prior agent reported the claude-in-chrome browser extension was not connected in its environment (tabs_context_mcp returned 'Browser extension is not connected'), so no navigation/scrubbing/screenshotting of the actual video was possible there, and it stopped rather than retry a dead channel. Its fallback text-provenance attempts also came up empty: WebFetch on the shorts URL returned only a truncated/empty body (consistent with Google bot-detection blocking direct watch-page fetches); two WebSearch queries combining creator name/title/subject returned only generic Lyngenfjord drone-tourism/stock-footage pages, with no creator commentary, interview, or press coverage describing cut count, shot length, or transition technique; and a noembed.com oEmbed proxy call returned 'no matching providers found'. This session independently repeated the check rather than trusting that report alone: a WebFetch of the live Shorts page and of the channel page both returned only truncated stubs with no extractable license, duration, or editorial content, and a fresh WebSearch ('"Above Horizon" drone YouTube channel Lyngen Alps fjord') returned only this same video as a search hit plus unrelated third-party Lyngenfjord drone-tourism pages -- no creator commentary of any kind. The only textual signal found in either session is the video's own title, 'Fjord One-Shot Drone' -- a promotional title claiming a 'one-shot' is not independent confirmation of actual shot count/continuity (this genre often disguises match-cuts within a nominal 'one shot'), so it is not treated as a genuine editorial observation. No cut count, shot length, or transition style can be honestly reported.
